definition: Sufficiently great or important to be worthy of attention
exampleSentence: The research revealed a significant correlation between sleep duration and academic performance.

definition: A result or effect of an action or condition
exampleSentence: The government failed to anticipate the economic consequences of the new policy.

definition: To show clearly and deliberately; to prove or make evident
exampleSentence: The experiment demonstrates that renewable energy can be economically viable.

definition: As a result; therefore
exampleSentence: The company failed to invest in new technology and consequently lost its competitive edge.

definition: Relating to the natural world and the impact of human activity on it
exampleSentence: Environmental concerns have led to stricter regulations on industrial emissions.

definition: To put a decision, plan, or agreement into effect
exampleSentence: The school decided to implement a new curriculum for science subjects.

definition: A fact or event that is observed to exist or happen, especially one whose cause is in question
exampleSentence: The phenomenon of urban migration has been widely studied by sociologists.

definition: A particular attitude toward or way of regarding something
exampleSentence: The article examines the issue from the perspective of small business owners.

definition: Of considerable importance, size, or worth
exampleSentence: The project received a substantial amount of funding from international donors.

definition: The act of obtaining something, such as knowledge, assets, or skills
exampleSentence: Language acquisition is most effective when learners are immersed in the target culture.

definition: To publicly recommend or support a particular policy or cause
exampleSentence: Many health experts advocate for stricter controls on sugar in processed foods.

definition: To distribute resources or duties for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The government must allocate more funds to public healthcare services.

definition: Detailed examination of the elements or structure of something
exampleSentence: A thorough analysis of the data revealed several unexpected trends.

definition: Roughly; about; used to show that something is almost but not completely accurate
exampleSentence: Approximately two-thirds of respondents indicated support for the new policy.

definition: A particular feature or part of something that is being considered
exampleSentence: One important aspect of the proposal is its potential impact on local employment.

definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature, ability, or quality of something
exampleSentence: Teachers assess students' progress through a combination of exams and coursework.

definition: A thing that is accepted as true without proof
exampleSentence: The study challenges the assumption that economic growth always leads to higher living standards.

definition: The power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience
exampleSentence: Local authorities are responsible for maintaining public parks and recreational facilities.

definition: Able to be used or obtained; at hand
exampleSentence: Scholarships are available for students from low-income backgrounds.

definition: Resulting in good; favorable or advantageous
exampleSentence: Regular physical activity is beneficial for both mental and physical health.

definition: The maximum amount that something can contain or produce
exampleSentence: The hospital's capacity has been expanded to accommodate the growing population.

definition: A class or division of people or things regarded as having particular shared characteristics
exampleSentence: The survey divided respondents into three categories based on their income levels.

definition: A task or situation that tests someone's abilities
exampleSentence: One of the main challenges facing the education system is the digital divide between students.

definition: A feature or quality belonging typically to a person, place, or thing
exampleSentence: Flexibility is a characteristic of successful organizations in rapidly changing markets.

definition: A condition or fact connected with an event or situation
exampleSentence: Due to unforeseen circumstances, the conference has been rescheduled to next month.

definition: The activity of buying and selling goods and services
exampleSentence: The rise of e-commerce has transformed traditional retail business models.

definition: The state of being dedicated to a cause, activity, or relationship
exampleSentence: The government's commitment to reducing carbon emissions was outlined in the recent policy document.

definition: A group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common
exampleSentence: Community engagement is essential for the success of urban development projects.

definition: Able to be likened to another thing; similar in quality or quantity
exampleSentence: The salaries offered in the public sector are not comparable to those in private industry.

definition: To pay or reward someone for work, loss, or inconvenience
exampleSentence: Workers were compensated for overtime hours at a higher rate.

definition: A part or element of a larger whole
exampleSentence: Each component of the ecosystem plays a vital role in maintaining balance.

definition: Including everything that is relevant; complete and thorough
exampleSentence: The report provides a comprehensive overview of climate change impacts on coastal regions.

definition: To focus one's attention or efforts on a particular activity
exampleSentence: Students find it difficult to concentrate in noisy classroom environments.

definition: An abstract idea or general notion
exampleSentence: The concept of sustainable development is central to modern environmental policy.

definition: To bring something to an end; to reach a judgment by reasoning
exampleSentence: The researchers concluded that diet plays a more significant role than exercise in weight management.

definition: To organize and carry out a particular activity or process
exampleSentence: The university will conduct a survey to gather student opinions on the new library hours.

definition: Feeling sure of oneself and one's abilities or judgment
exampleSentence: Students who are confident in their speaking abilities tend to perform better in oral exams.

definition: A serious disagreement or argument, or a clash between opposing principles
exampleSentence: The conflict between economic development and environmental protection is a recurring theme in politics.

definition: Aware of and responding to one's surroundings or a particular fact
exampleSentence: Modern consumers are increasingly conscious of the environmental impact of their purchases.

definition: To be composed or made up of particular elements
exampleSentence: The course consists of ten modules, each focusing on a different aspect of business management.

definition: Acting or done in the same way over time; unchanging
exampleSentence: Consistent practice is key to improving language proficiency.

definition: To be a part of a whole; to make up or form something
exampleSentence: International students constitute approximately twenty percent of the university's population.

definition: To seek information or advice from a person or source
exampleSentence: Before making major investments, companies should consult with financial advisors.

definition: To eat, drink, or use up a resource
exampleSentence: Households in developed countries consume far more energy than those in developing nations.

definition: Living or occurring at the same time; current or modern
exampleSentence: Contemporary art often challenges traditional notions of beauty and meaning.

definition: To give something, such as money, time, or effort, to a common supply or purpose
exampleSentence: Several factors contribute to the rising cost of higher education.

definition: A prolonged public disagreement or debate about a matter of opinion
exampleSentence: The construction of the new highway has sparked controversy among environmental groups.

definition: A way of behaving that is considered usual or proper in a society or group
exampleSentence: Social conventions vary greatly from one culture to another.

definition: To cause someone to believe firmly in the truth of something
exampleSentence: The presenter used statistical evidence to convince the audience of the need for action.

definition: To work jointly toward the same end
exampleSentence: Countries must cooperate more effectively to address global environmental issues.

definition: Matching or connected with something that has just been mentioned
exampleSentence: Sales increased in the first quarter, with a corresponding rise in profits.

definition: A principle or standard by which something may be judged or decided
exampleSentence: The main criterion for admission to the program is academic excellence.

definition: Decisive or critical, especially in the success or failure of something
exampleSentence: Early childhood education plays a crucial role in cognitive development.

definition: The subjects making up a course of study in a school or college
exampleSentence: The school revised its curriculum to include more digital literacy training.

definition: A formal discussion on a particular topic in which opposing arguments are put forward
exampleSentence: The debate over genetically modified foods continues to divide public opinion.

definition: To decrease in quantity, importance, or quality; to become smaller or weaker
exampleSentence: The number of students choosing to study humanities has declined sharply over the past decade.

definition: To devote time, effort, or resources to a particular task or purpose
exampleSentence: The research center is dedicated to finding sustainable solutions for urban transport.

definition: Clearly stated, decided, or known; not vague
exampleSentence: There is a definite link between smoking and lung cancer, supported by extensive research.

definition: Laws collectively, or the process of making laws
exampleSentence: New legislation on data privacy will come into effect next year.

definition: Relating to the structure of populations, including age, income, and education
exampleSentence: The marketing campaign was tailored to appeal to a younger demographic.

definition: Contingent on or determined by something else
exampleSentence: Many developing countries remain heavily dependent on agricultural exports.

definition: To obtain something from a specified source
exampleSentence: Many English words are derived from Latin and Greek origins.

definition: Without being affected by; in spite of
exampleSentence: Despite the heavy rain, the outdoor concert continued as planned.

definition: To cause something to happen in a particular way; to decide or settle
exampleSentence: Several factors determine a student's success in higher education.

definition: Relating to countries with less advanced economies and lower income levels
exampleSentence: Microfinance has helped many entrepreneurs in developing countries start small businesses.

definition: A piece of equipment or machinery designed to serve a specific purpose
exampleSentence: Electronic devices such as smartphones and tablets have transformed the way we communicate.

definition: A certificate awarded by an educational institution for completing a course of study
exampleSentence: He earned a diploma in graphic design before starting his own studio.

definition: A branch of knowledge, or the practice of training people to obey rules
exampleSentence: Economics is a discipline that requires both analytical and quantitative skills.

definition: Unjust treatment of people based on their group, class, or category
exampleSentence: Anti-discrimination laws aim to ensure equal opportunities for all job applicants.

definition: Intended to be used once and then thrown away
exampleSentence: The widespread use of disposable plastic products has created a serious waste management crisis.

definition: Recognizably different in nature from something else of a similar type
exampleSentence: The study identified three distinct stages of language acquisition in children.

definition: To recognize or point out a difference between two or more things
exampleSentence: It is important to distinguish between facts and opinions when analyzing a text.

definition: To give or spread out over a wide area or among a group
exampleSentence: Aid agencies distributed food and medicine to families affected by the earthquake.

definition: Showing a great deal of variety; very different
exampleSentence: Universities today have more diverse student populations than ever before.

definition: Relating to a particular country's internal affairs; relating to the home
exampleSentence: The government's domestic policies focus on improving healthcare and education.

definition: To have a commanding influence over; to be the most important or conspicuous
exampleSentence: Technology companies dominate the stock market, accounting for a large share of total value.

definition: Sudden and striking; relating to drama or performance
exampleSentence: There has been a dramatic increase in online learning since the pandemic.

definition: The time during which something continues or lasts
exampleSentence: The duration of the course is twelve weeks, with classes held twice a week.

definition: Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress
exampleSentence: The job market is dynamic, requiring workers to continuously update their skills.

definition: Giving good value for the money spent; sparing in use of resources
exampleSentence: Electric cars are more economical to run than traditional petrol vehicles.

definition: To prepare written material for publication by correcting and revising it
exampleSentence: The editor spent hours carefully editing the manuscript before it went to print.

definition: To completely remove or get rid of something
exampleSentence: The new policy aims to eliminate poverty by providing universal basic income.

definition: Newly created or becoming prominent
exampleSentence: Emerging economies such as India and Brazil are driving global growth.

definition: Special importance or value placed on something
exampleSentence: The school places a strong emphasis on developing critical thinking skills.

definition: To unexpectedly experience or be faced with something
exampleSentence: Tourists may encounter language barriers when traveling to remote regions.

definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent
exampleSentence: The construction project required an enormous investment of both time and money.

definition: To make certain that something will happen or be the case
exampleSentence: Schools must ensure that all students have equal access to educational resources.

definition: A business or company, or a project or undertaking that involves risk
exampleSentence: Small and medium-sized enterprises form the backbone of the national economy.

definition: Equal in value, amount, function, or meaning
exampleSentence: A bachelor's degree from this university is equivalent to a British honours degree.

definition: Absolutely necessary; extremely important
exampleSentence: Good communication skills are essential for success in any professional field.

definition: To set up on a firm or permanent basis; to initiate or create
exampleSentence: The organization was established in 1995 to promote cross-cultural understanding.

definition: To judge or determine the significance, worth, or quality of something
exampleSentence: Researchers are evaluating the effectiveness of the new teaching method.

definition: Plain or clear; easily seen or understood
exampleSentence: The effects of climate change are becoming increasingly evident across the globe.

definition: The gradual development of something over time
exampleSentence: The evolution of digital technology has transformed almost every aspect of modern life.

definition: To inspect or scrutinize closely to determine condition or quality
exampleSentence: The study examines the relationship between social media use and mental health.

definition: To be greater in number or degree than a limit or expectation
exampleSentence: Demand for renewable energy has exceeded all previous forecasts.

definition: To show or display something publicly; to manifest a quality or feeling
exampleSentence: The museum will exhibit artefacts from ancient Egyptian civilization.

definition: To become or make larger in size, scope, or amount
exampleSentence: The company plans to expand its operations into Asian markets next year.

definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion
exampleSentence: The instructions were explicit about the safety procedures to be followed.

definition: To make full use of a resource or advantage, sometimes unfairly
exampleSentence: Companies should exploit technological advances to improve their environmental performance.

definition: The state of being in contact with or subjected to something
exampleSentence: Prolonged exposure to air pollution can lead to serious respiratory problems.

definition: Covering or affecting a large area; large in amount or scale
exampleSentence: The earthquake caused extensive damage to buildings and infrastructure.

definition: Belonging to or affecting the outside of something; coming from outside
exampleSentence: The university relies on external funding for its research programs.

definition: To make an action or process easier or more likely to happen
exampleSentence: Online platforms facilitate communication between students and teachers outside the classroom.

definition: Possible and practical to achieve easily
exampleSentence: The engineers concluded that the proposed bridge design was technically feasible.

definition: In a way that is appropriate to the particular circumstances
exampleSentence: The situation has changed, and we must act accordingly.

definition: The center of interest or activity; concentrated attention
exampleSentence: The main focus of the conference is sustainable urban development.

definition: To predict or estimate a future event or trend
exampleSentence: Economists forecast that inflation will remain stable for the rest of the year.

definition: A method, principle, or rule for achieving something
exampleSentence: There is no simple formula for success in business, but hard work is essential.

definition: About to appear or become available; ready to be given
exampleSentence: Details of the new policy will be announced in the forthcoming government report.

definition: The basis or groundwork of something; an institution established with an endowment
exampleSentence: A strong foundation in mathematics is essential for studying engineering.

definition: Occurring or done many times in a short period
exampleSentence: Frequent exercise is recommended for maintaining cardiovascular health.

definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
exampleSentence: Access to education is a fundamental human right that should be available to all.

definition: To produce or create something, especially power, ideas, or income
exampleSentence: The wind farm is capable of generating enough electricity to power 50,000 homes.

definition: Truly what something is said to be; authentic and sincere
exampleSentence: The charity showed genuine concern for the welfare of the local community.

definition: Relating to the whole world; worldwide in scope or application
exampleSentence: Climate change is a global issue that requires international cooperation.

definition: Taking place slowly or by degrees over a period of time
exampleSentence: There has been a gradual improvement in air quality since the introduction of emissions controls.

definition: To formally promise or ensure that something will happen or be done
exampleSentence: Most manufacturers guarantee their products for at least one year.

definition: As a consequence; for this reason
exampleSentence: The company failed to innovate and hence lost its market dominance.

definition: To emphasize or draw special attention to something
exampleSentence: The report highlights the urgent need for investment in public transportation.

definition: A proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence as a starting point for investigation
exampleSentence: The researchers tested their hypothesis by conducting a series of controlled experiments.

definition: Exactly the same in every respect
exampleSentence: The two samples were almost identical under microscopic examination.

definition: To establish who or what something is; to recognize or pinpoint
exampleSentence: Scientists have identified a potential link between air pollution and cognitive decline.

definition: So as to give the impression of having a certain quality; apparently
exampleSentence: The seemingly simple problem turned out to be quite complex.

definition: To explain or clarify by providing examples, pictures, or diagrams
exampleSentence: The graph illustrates the steady decline in manufacturing jobs over the past twenty years.

definition: A person who comes to live permanently in a foreign country
exampleSentence: Immigrants have made significant contributions to the cultural and economic life of the country.

definition: The strong effect or influence that something has on a situation or person
exampleSentence: The new policy will have a significant impact on small businesses.

definition: A possible consequence or effect of an action or decision
exampleSentence: The study has important implications for how we treat patients with chronic illnesses.

definition: To force something unwelcome to be accepted or done
exampleSentence: The government plans to impose stricter regulations on plastic waste disposal.

definition: Something that motivates or encourages someone to do something
exampleSentence: Tax incentives are often used to encourage businesses to invest in renewable energy.

definition: An event or occurrence, especially one that is unusual or unpleasant
exampleSentence: Fortunately, no one was injured in the industrial incident at the factory.

definition: To include or integrate something as part of a whole
exampleSentence: The new curriculum incorporates practical skills training alongside theoretical knowledge.

definition: To point out or show; to be a sign of something
exampleSentence: Preliminary results indicate that the new drug is effective in reducing symptoms.

definition: Relating to a single person or thing as distinct from a group
exampleSentence: Each student receives an individual learning plan tailored to their needs.

definition: Certain to happen; unavoidable
exampleSentence: Change is inevitable in any growing organization, and adaptability is key.

definition: The capacity to have an effect on the character, development, or behavior of someone or something
exampleSentence: Social media has a powerful influence on young people's purchasing decisions.

definition: A new plan or action to address a problem or achieve a goal
exampleSentence: The government launched a new initiative to promote recycling in urban areas.

definition: A new method, idea, or product, or the act of introducing something new
exampleSentence: Technological innovation has revolutionized the healthcare industry in recent decades.

definition: By that means; as a result of that
exampleSentence: The company reduced costs, thereby increasing its profit margin.

definition: An established organization, especially one providing public services or education
exampleSentence: The university is one of the oldest academic institutions in the country.

definition: As is certain to happen; unavoidably
exampleSentence: Rapid technological change inevitably leads to job displacement in some sectors.

definition: To combine two or more things to form a whole
exampleSentence: The school aims to integrate technology into every aspect of the learning process.

definition: Extreme in degree, strength, or effect
exampleSentence: The intense competition for university places puts enormous pressure on students.

definition: To communicate or be involved with others; to act in a way that affects another
exampleSentence: Children learn social skills by interacting with their peers in group activities.

definition: In a permanent, essential, or characteristic way
exampleSentence: Some risks are inherently associated with any investment.

definition: To explain the meaning of something; to understand in a particular way
exampleSentence: The data can be interpreted in several different ways, depending on the theoretical framework.

definition: To take action in order to prevent or alter a result or course of events
exampleSentence: The government had to intervene to prevent the collapse of the banking system.

definition: By a strikingly large amount; to a very great degree
exampleSentence: Online shopping has grown dramatically over the past decade.

definition: To carry out a systematic or formal inquiry to discover and examine facts
exampleSentence: The committee was formed to investigate the causes of the industrial accident.

definition: To cause someone or something to be alone, separate, or apart from others
exampleSentence: Patients with contagious diseases are isolated to prevent the spread of infection.

definition: An important topic or problem for debate or discussion
exampleSentence: Climate change is one of the most pressing issues facing our generation.

definition: To show or prove to be right or reasonable
exampleSentence: The benefits of the project must justify the costs involved.

definition: A classifying phrase or name applied to a person or thing
exampleSentence: Food products must display clear labels indicating their nutritional content.

definition: To start or set in motion an activity, product, or organization
exampleSentence: The company plans to launch its new smartphone model in March.

definition: A sheet or thickness of material covering a surface; a level or stratum
exampleSentence: The ozone layer protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation.

definition: Relating to the law; permitted or required by law
exampleSentence: The company is required to comply with all legal regulations regarding data protection.

definition: Open to new ideas; favoring individual freedom and moderate reform
exampleSentence: The country has adopted more liberal immigration policies in recent years.

definition: In the same way; similarly
exampleSentence: The first experiment failed, and the second was likewise unsuccessful.

definition: A point beyond which something cannot go
exampleSentence: There is a limit to how much information the human brain can process at once.

definition: To keep something in good condition or at the same level
exampleSentence: Regular exercise is important for maintaining good physical health.

definition: Important, serious, or significant in size or degree
exampleSentence: The government announced major reforms to the healthcare system.

definition: To handle or control something skillfully, often in a way that is unfair
exampleSentence: Advertisers often manipulate consumer behavior through carefully crafted messages.

definition: To produce goods on a large scale using machinery
exampleSentence: The company manufactures electronic components in factories across Southeast Asia.

definition: The edge or border of something; the difference between cost and selling price
exampleSentence: Profit margins have been shrinking due to rising raw material costs.

definition: In a general way; without detail; widely
exampleSentence: The term 'sustainable development' is broadly used across many disciplines.

definition: Fully developed physically or mentally; adult or ripe
exampleSentence: The mature students brought valuable work experience to the classroom discussions.

definition: As great, high, or intense as possible or permitted
exampleSentence: The maximum temperature recorded during the heatwave was forty degrees Celsius.

definition: A means of communicating information; a middle point between extremes
exampleSentence: Television remains a powerful medium for advertising despite the rise of digital platforms.

definition: Relating to the mind or the process of thinking
exampleSentence: There is growing awareness of the importance of mental health in the workplace.

definition: A particular way of doing something, especially a systematic procedure
exampleSentence: The study compares different teaching methods to determine which is most effective.

definition: Only just; almost not
exampleSentence: The government's budget barely covers essential public services.

definition: Relating to the armed forces or to war
exampleSentence: Military spending accounts for a significant portion of the national budget.

definition: The smallest or least amount possible or required
exampleSentence: A minimum of three years of experience is required for this position.

definition: To make partial changes to something to improve it or adapt it
exampleSentence: The design was modified to meet the specific needs of elderly users.

definition: In a way that is striking or deserving of attention
exampleSentence: Several factors contributed to the decline, most notably the rise in production costs.

definition: Concerned with the principles of right and wrong behavior
exampleSentence: Companies have a moral responsibility to treat their workers fairly.

definition: Experienced or done by two or more parties equally; reciprocal
exampleSentence: The two countries signed a mutual defense agreement to protect each other's interests.

definition: To a very great extent; extremely
exampleSentence: The tourism industry has grown tremendously since the airport was built.

definition: To try to reach an agreement through discussion and compromise
exampleSentence: Union representatives are negotiating with management for better working conditions.

definition: Introducing a statement that reverses the previous one
exampleSentence: Urban areas often have higher crime rates; conversely, they offer more employment opportunities.

definition: A standard or pattern of behavior that is typical or expected
exampleSentence: Working from home has become the norm for many office workers since the pandemic.

definition: Until now; until the point in time under discussion
exampleSentence: The report reveals information that was hitherto unavailable to the public.

definition: Great in number; many
exampleSentence: Numerous studies have confirmed the link between exercise and improved mental health.

definition: To get something, especially by making an effort
exampleSentence: It is becoming increasingly difficult to obtain a mortgage without a stable income.

definition: Easily perceived or understood; clear and apparent
exampleSentence: The benefits of recycling are obvious, yet many people still fail to do it.

definition: In a strict manner; rigorously; exclusively
exampleSentence: The interview is strictly confidential and will not be shared with anyone.

definition: Entirely; completely; to the fullest extent
exampleSentence: The committee was not wholly convinced by the arguments presented.

definition: Continuing to exist or develop; in progress
exampleSentence: There is an ongoing debate about the effectiveness of standardized testing.

definition: A thing that may be chosen; a choice or alternative
exampleSentence: Students have the option of studying abroad for one semester as part of their degree.

definition: A part of the body that performs a specific function
exampleSentence: The heart is the most vital organ in the human circulatory system.

definition: The result or consequence of an action, process, or situation
exampleSentence: The outcome of the election will have far-reaching implications for economic policy.

definition: In a literal manner or sense; exactly; used for emphasis
exampleSentence: The number of applicants has literally doubled since last year.

definition: Taken as a whole; in general
exampleSentence: Overall, the conference was considered a great success by participants.

definition: In every case; without exception; always
exampleSentence: The bus is invariably late during the morning rush hour.

definition: In or to a foreign country across the sea
exampleSentence: Many students choose to study overseas to gain international experience.

definition: To take part or become involved in an activity
exampleSentence: Students are encouraged to participate in extracurricular activities to develop soft skills.

definition: A formal association between two or more parties for mutual benefit
exampleSentence: The university formed a partnership with local businesses to provide internship opportunities.

definition: Strong and barely controllable emotion or enthusiasm
exampleSentence: Her passion for environmental conservation inspired her to study ecology.

definition: Without human intervention; as a necessary result
exampleSentence: The system automatically backs up all files at the end of each day.

definition: To a moderate degree as compared to something else; relatively
exampleSentence: The city is comparatively safe when measured against other major urban centers.

definition: A person of the same age, status, or ability as another
exampleSentence: Peer pressure can significantly influence teenagers' decisions and behavior.

definition: By a notably large amount or to a notably large extent
exampleSentence: The new treatment is considerably more effective than previous options.

definition: To become aware of something through the senses; to see or understand
exampleSentence: How consumers perceive a brand can significantly affect their purchasing decisions.

definition: Extremely; to a very great degree
exampleSentence: The examination was exceedingly difficult, with a pass rate of only thirty percent.

definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model or framework
exampleSentence: The discovery marked a paradigm shift in our understanding of the universe.

definition: The study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, and ethics
exampleSentence: The company's business philosophy emphasizes customer satisfaction above all else.

definition: Relating to the body as opposed to the mind; material or tangible
exampleSentence: Regular physical activity reduces the risk of developing chronic diseases.

definition: More and more; to an ever-increasing extent
exampleSentence: Employers are increasingly valuing soft skills alongside technical qualifications.

definition: To have or own something as a characteristic or property
exampleSentence: The candidate possesses excellent communication and leadership skills.

definition: Latent qualities or abilities that may be developed in the future
exampleSentence: The region has enormous potential for renewable energy generation.

definition: To a great extent; mostly or mainly
exampleSentence: The success of the project was largely due to effective teamwork.

definition: Only; just; simply; and nothing more
exampleSentence: This is merely a temporary setback; the long-term outlook remains positive.

definition: Marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail
exampleSentence: The instructions must be precise to ensure the experiment can be replicated.

definition: To say or estimate that a specified thing will happen in the future
exampleSentence: Economists predict that inflation will rise to four percent by the end of the year.

definition: Mainly; for the most part
exampleSentence: The region is predominantly agricultural, with few industrial facilities.

definition: That is to say; specifically (used to introduce a more precise term)
exampleSentence: Three factors explain the decline, namely rising costs, falling demand, and increased competition.

definition: Denoting an action or event preceding the main one; initial
exampleSentence: Preliminary findings suggest that the drug may have significant benefits for patients.

definition: To maintain something in its original or existing state; to protect
exampleSentence: National parks were established to preserve natural habitats for future generations.

definition: Only in part; to a limited extent
exampleSentence: The project was only partially successful due to budget constraints.

definition: Existing or occurring before in time or order
exampleSentence: The current policy has failed where previous attempts succeeded.

definition: Of chief importance; principal; first in order or development
exampleSentence: The primary objective of the program is to reduce unemployment among young people.

definition: A fundamental truth or proposition serving as the foundation for a system of belief
exampleSentence: The principle of equality is enshrined in the country's constitution.

definition: A thing regarded as more important than others
exampleSentence: Improving public healthcare should be a top priority for any government.

definition: Only just; almost not; certainly not
exampleSentence: There is scarcely any evidence to support the alternative hypothesis.

definition: At the present time; now; soon
exampleSentence: The company is presently developing a new range of eco-friendly products.

definition: A series of actions or steps taken to achieve a particular end
exampleSentence: The application process for university can be lengthy and requires careful planning.

definition: Relating to a job that requires specialized training and formal qualifications
exampleSentence: The course is designed to help students develop professional skills for the workplace.

definition: Very great or intense; having deep meaning or significance
exampleSentence: The internet has had a profound impact on the way we access information.

definition: At an earlier time; before the present time
exampleSentence: The study confirmed findings that were previously reported by other researchers.

definition: To support or actively encourage something; to raise to a higher position
exampleSentence: The campaign aims to promote healthy eating habits among school children.

definition: In a gradual or step-by-step manner
exampleSentence: The symptoms became progressively worse over the course of several months.

definition: A part, share, or number considered in comparative relation to a whole
exampleSentence: A large proportion of the population lives in urban areas.

definition: According to what is said or reported
exampleSentence: The company is reportedly planning to open a new factory in Eastern Europe.

definition: The state of being wealthy and successful
exampleSentence: Economic prosperity has led to improved living standards across the country.

definition: Relating to the mind or mental processes
exampleSentence: Chronic stress can have serious psychological consequences if left untreated.

definition: To prepare and issue book, article, or other material for public distribution
exampleSentence: The researcher hopes to publish her findings in a leading scientific journal.

definition: Approximately; in a manner lacking refinement or precision
exampleSentence: Roughly three-quarters of respondents supported the proposed changes.

definition: A pass of an examination or an official completion of a course
exampleSentence: The job advertisement requires applicants to have a relevant qualification in finance.

definition: To repeat or copy words from another source, usually with acknowledgment
exampleSentence: The article quotes several experts who support the proposed policy changes.

definition: Relating to or affecting the fundamental nature of something; far-reaching
exampleSentence: The country needs radical reform of its education system to compete globally.

definition: The area of variation between limits; a set of different things of the same type
exampleSentence: The university offers a wide range of courses in science and humanities.

definition: After a particular thing has happened; afterwards
exampleSentence: The product was launched in Europe and subsequently introduced to Asian markets.

definition: A measure, quantity, or frequency measured against another quantity
exampleSentence: The unemployment rate has fallen to its lowest level in a decade.

definition: The quantitative relation between two amounts showing the number of times one contains the other
exampleSentence: The student-to-teacher ratio in this school is fifteen to one.

definition: Based on reason rather than emotion; logical and sensible
exampleSentence: A rational approach to problem-solving involves gathering data before making decisions.

definition: Finally; in the end; when all factors are considered
exampleSentence: Ultimately, the success of the project depends on the quality of the team.

definition: Without hesitation or difficulty; willingly
exampleSentence: Information is readily available online, making research easier than ever before.

definition: Having a sensible and practical view of what can be achieved
exampleSentence: Students should set realistic goals for their academic performance.

definition: A period of temporary economic decline during which trade and industrial activity are reduced
exampleSentence: The country experienced a severe recession following the financial crisis.

definition: Close to the side of; next to; together with
exampleSentence: The new policy will operate alongside existing regulations.

definition: To suggest that someone or something would be good or suitable for a purpose
exampleSentence: Doctors recommend that adults get at least thirty minutes of exercise daily.

definition: To return to a normal state after a setback, illness, or difficulty
exampleSentence: The economy is expected to recover slowly over the next two years.

definition: To mention or allude to something; to consult a source for information
exampleSentence: The study refers to previous research conducted in the same field.

definition: Surrounded by; in the middle of a particular situation
exampleSentence: The company continued to grow amid the economic downturn.

definition: To make changes to something in order to improve it
exampleSentence: The government plans to reform the healthcare system to make it more efficient.

definition: Without paying attention to the present situation; despite the circumstances
exampleSentence: Regardless of their background, all students should have access to quality education.

definition: An area or division of a country or the world with specific characteristics
exampleSentence: Coastal regions are particularly vulnerable to the effects of rising sea levels.

definition: To record or enroll officially; to detect or show something
exampleSentence: Students must register for courses before the beginning of each semester.

definition: To control or maintain the rate or speed of something; to impose rules
exampleSentence: The government regulates the pharmaceutical industry to ensure drug safety.

definition: In the midst of; surrounded by; in the group of
exampleSentence: The theory is widely accepted amongst academic circles.

definition: To refuse to accept, consider, or submit to something
exampleSentence: The committee rejected the proposal due to insufficient evidence.

definition: In addition to; apart from
exampleSentence: Besides tuition fees, students must also budget for accommodation and books.

definition: Considered in relation to something else; comparative
exampleSentence: The relative importance of different factors varies depending on the context.

definition: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand

definition: Underneath; at a lower level or position
exampleSentence: The archaeological remains were discovered buried beneath the city streets.

definition: To depend on with full trust or confidence
exampleSentence: Developing countries often rely heavily on agriculture for economic growth.

definition: Outside the limits or scope of; further away than

definition: Worthy of attention; striking and extraordinary
exampleSentence: The scientist made a remarkable discovery that changed our understanding of genetics.

definition: A solution or treatment for a problem, disease, or difficulty
exampleSentence: There is no quick remedy for the complex problems facing the education system.

definition: Far away in distance, time, or relation; isolated
exampleSentence: Remote work has become increasingly common since technological advances made it feasible.

definition: About; regarding; on the subject of

definition: Capable of being replenished naturally, such as energy from the sun or wind
exampleSentence: Investment in renewable energy has grown substantially over the past decade.

definition: In spite of; despite; without being affected by
exampleSentence: Notwithstanding the difficulties, the team managed to complete the project on schedule.

definition: To stand for or symbolize; to act as a delegate on behalf of others
exampleSentence: The graph represents the change in temperature over the past century.

definition: The beliefs or opinions generally held about someone or something
exampleSentence: The company has an excellent reputation for customer service and product quality.

definition: Not including; except for
exampleSentence: The price is two hundred dollars, excluding delivery charges.

definition: Systematic investigation to establish facts and reach new conclusions
exampleSentence: Further research is needed to fully understand the long-term effects of the treatment.

definition: To keep something for future use; to arrange for a seat or ticket in advance
exampleSentence: The hotel reserves the right to cancel bookings without compensation.

definition: To live in a particular place permanently or for a long period
exampleSentence: A large number of international students reside in university accommodation.

definition: After; as a result of
exampleSentence: Following the meeting, a summary will be distributed to all participants.

definition: To withstand the action or effect of something; to oppose actively
exampleSentence: Some communities initially resisted the introduction of new technology.

definition: Containing as part of the whole being considered
exampleSentence: The package includes several services, including free technical support.

definition: Within; in the inner part of
exampleSentence: The documents must be submitted inside the specified deadline.

definition: Relating separately to each of the people or things previously mentioned
exampleSentence: After graduation, the students returned to their respective home countries.

definition: Less; with the deduction of
exampleSentence: The net profit is revenue minus operating expenses and taxes.

definition: To bring back to a former condition, place, or position
exampleSentence: Conservationists are working to restore the natural habitat of the endangered species.

definition: To limit or confine someone or something; to keep within certain bounds
exampleSentence: New laws restrict the amount of waste that factories can discharge into rivers.

definition: From this time onward; from now on
exampleSentence: Henceforth, all employees must submit their reports by Friday each week.

definition: Situated on the other side of; facing
exampleSentence: The new library is located opposite the main campus entrance.

definition: Beyond the boundaries or limits of
exampleSentence: The issue falls outside the scope of this investigation.

definition: With the addition of; and also
exampleSentence: The cost of the materials plus labor amounts to over five thousand dollars.

definition: A dramatic and wide-reaching change in conditions or systems
exampleSentence: The industrial revolution transformed society from agrarian to manufacturing-based.

definition: Extremely thorough, exhaustive, or accurate
exampleSentence: All products undergo rigorous testing before they are released to the market.

definition: The function or part played by a person or thing in a particular situation
exampleSentence: Technology plays a vital role in modern education.

definition: With respect to; concerning
exampleSentence: The committee released a statement regarding the proposed changes.

definition: In or to every part of; during the entire period
exampleSentence: The university has branches throughout the country.

definition: The relative size or extent of something; a graduated range or series
exampleSentence: The scale of the environmental damage caused by the oil spill was enormous.

definition: In the direction of; as part of the process of achieving
exampleSentence: The government has taken significant steps toward reducing carbon emissions.

definition: Against; in contrast to; as opposed to
exampleSentence: The debate centered on the benefits of public versus private education.

definition: Inside; not beyond; occurring inside a particular period
exampleSentence: All assignments must be submitted within one week of the deadline.

definition: A specialist in a particular branch of study, especially the humanities
exampleSentence: The visiting scholar gave a fascinating lecture on medieval history.

definition: The state of being free from danger or threat; safety measures
exampleSentence: Job security is a growing concern for workers in the gig economy.

definition: An attempt to achieve a goal; a serious undertaking
exampleSentence: Starting a new business is a risky but potentially rewarding endeavor.

definition: A sudden, dramatic, and important discovery or development
exampleSentence: The medical breakthrough could lead to new treatments for the disease.

definition: A particular order in which related events or things follow each other
exampleSentence: The DNA sequence determines the genetic characteristics of an organism.

definition: Very serious or strict; extremely intense or harsh
exampleSentence: The region experienced severe drought conditions for three consecutive years.

definition: To change position or direction; to move from one place to another
exampleSentence: Public opinion has shifted significantly in favor of renewable energy.

definition: Worthy of attention or notice; remarkable
exampleSentence: The city has made notable progress in reducing air pollution over the past decade.

definition: General agreement among a group of people
exampleSentence: There is a growing consensus among scientists that urgent action is needed.

definition: Complex or refined; developed to a high degree of complexity
exampleSentence: The hospital uses sophisticated equipment to diagnose rare diseases.

definition: A place, person, or thing from which something originates or is obtained
exampleSentence: Renewable energy sources like solar and wind are becoming more affordable.

definition: Clearly defined or identified; precise and particular
exampleSentence: The doctor gave specific instructions regarding the patient's diet and exercise.

definition: A limitation or restriction on something
exampleSentence: Budget constraints prevented the university from hiring additional staff.

definition: Firmly fixed; not likely to change or fail
exampleSentence: A stable economy is essential for attracting foreign investment.

definition: A level of quality or achievement that is considered acceptable or desirable
exampleSentence: The school maintains high academic standards across all subjects.

definition: The relative social, professional, or legal position of someone or something
exampleSentence: The employment status of graduates is tracked for six months after completion.

definition: In a regular and continuous manner; at a constant rate
exampleSentence: The number of students enrolling in online courses has increased steadily.

definition: A situation requiring a difficult choice between two undesirable options
exampleSentence: Workers face the dilemma of either accepting lower wages or losing their jobs.

definition: A plan of action designed to achieve a long-term or overall aim
exampleSentence: The company developed a new marketing strategy to reach younger consumers.

definition: A great difference between things; inequality
exampleSentence: The report highlights the growing disparity between rich and poor communities.

definition: The arrangement of and relations between the parts or elements of something
exampleSentence: The essay has a clear structure with an introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion.

definition: To present or hand in something for consideration or judgment
exampleSentence: Students must submit their assignments before the deadline.

definition: A heated disagreement or argument between parties
exampleSentence: The dispute between management and workers was resolved through mediation.

definition: A sum of money granted by the government to support an industry or activity
exampleSentence: The government provides subsidies to farmers to ensure food security.

definition: A notable difference between similar things; excellence
exampleSentence: It is important to draw a clear distinction between facts and opinions.

definition: Enough to meet a need or purpose; adequate
exampleSentence: There is not sufficient evidence to support the claim that the drug is harmful.

definition: To give a brief statement of the main points of something
exampleSentence: The final paragraph should summarize the main arguments presented in the essay.

definition: Higher in rank, status, or quality; better than average
exampleSentence: The product is superior to its competitors in terms of durability.

definition: A set of beliefs or principles held by a group
exampleSentence: The company's doctrine emphasizes innovation and customer satisfaction.

definition: A systematic examination or study of people's opinions or conditions
exampleSentence: According to a recent survey, more than seventy percent of adults support the new policy.

definition: To continue to live or exist in spite of danger or hardship
exampleSentence: Small businesses often struggle to survive during economic recessions.

definition: An area of territory or knowledge under one's control
exampleSentence: Quantum physics is a highly specialized domain of scientific research.

definition: To temporarily stop or delay something; to hang from something
exampleSentence: The airline suspended all flights due to the volcanic ash cloud.

definition: Capable of being maintained at a steady level without exhausting resources
exampleSentence: The city is investing in sustainable transport solutions like bike-sharing schemes.

definition: A thing that represents or stands for something else, especially a material object representing an abstract idea
exampleSentence: The dove is a universal symbol of peace and harmony.

definition: Feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune
exampleSentence: The public expressed sympathy for the families affected by the tragedy.

definition: A disadvantage or negative feature of something
exampleSentence: The main drawback of living in a big city is the high cost of housing.

definition: An objective or goal; a person or thing aimed at
exampleSentence: The company has set ambitious sales targets for the upcoming financial year.

definition: A piece of work to be done or undertaken
exampleSentence: Completing the group task required effective communication and collaboration.

definition: A way of carrying out a particular task, especially the execution of an artistic or scientific work
exampleSentence: The artist developed a unique painting technique using natural pigments.

definition: Lasting for only a limited period of time; not permanent
exampleSentence: Many students take temporary jobs during summer vacations to earn extra money.

definition: An inclination or natural way of behaving; a trend
exampleSentence: There is a growing tendency for people to work from home rather than commute.

definition: An intervening time or space between events
exampleSentence: The survey was conducted at regular intervals over a five-year period.

definition: Relating to the end of something; fatal or incurable
exampleSentence: The patient was diagnosed with a terminal illness and given six months to live.

definition: An area of land under the jurisdiction of a ruler or state
exampleSentence: The company expanded into new territory by opening offices in Southeast Asia.

definition: Concerned with the theory of a subject rather than its practical application
exampleSentence: The course provides a balance of theoretical knowledge and practical skills.

definition: The complete removal or destruction of something
exampleSentence: The elimination of plastic waste will require significant changes in consumer behavior.

definition: Complete with regard to every detail; careful and meticulous
exampleSentence: The report was based on a thorough analysis of all available data.

definition: An indication of something undesirable about to happen; a person or thing likely to cause damage
exampleSentence: Climate change poses a serious threat to coastal communities worldwide.

definition: The ability to understand and share the feelings of others
exampleSentence: Effective leaders demonstrate empathy toward their team members.

definition: A subject of discussion, writing, or conversation

definition: Intense and eager enjoyment or interest in something
exampleSentence: The students showed great enthusiasm for the interactive learning activities.

definition: A long-established custom or belief passed from generation to generation
exampleSentence: The country has a rich tradition of storytelling and folk music.

definition: The intrinsic nature or indispensable quality of something
exampleSentence: The essence of the argument is that education should be accessible to all.

definition: Specialized knowledge or skill in a particular field
exampleSentence: The consultant brought valuable expertise in supply chain management.

definition: A short passage taken from a text or piece of music
exampleSentence: The reading passage is an extract from a novel published in 1995.

definition: A small part or amount of something
exampleSentence: Only a fraction of applicants are accepted into the competitive program.

definition: Conflict or disagreement between people or groups
exampleSentence: There has been friction between the two departments over resource allocation.

definition: A general direction in which something is developing or changing
exampleSentence: The trend toward urbanization shows no signs of slowing down.

definition: The main or essential part of a matter; the general meaning
exampleSentence: The introductory paragraph explains the gist of the argument.

definition: Having the distinctive qualities of a particular type; characteristic
exampleSentence: A typical day for a university student involves attending lectures and studying independently.

definition: A firm understanding of something; the ability to comprehend
exampleSentence: Students need a solid grasp of basic concepts before moving to advanced topics.

definition: To experience or be subjected to something, especially a change or treatment
exampleSentence: The patient had to undergo surgery to repair the damaged ligament.

definition: Suffering or difficulty caused by unfavorable circumstances
exampleSentence: Many families experienced severe financial hardship during the recession.

definition: To commit to and begin a project or responsibility
exampleSentence: The university will undertake a major renovation of the library facilities.

definition: A system in which people or things are ranked according to status or authority
exampleSentence: The corporate hierarchy determines the chain of command within the organization.

definition: Affecting or relating to all people or things in the world
exampleSentence: Access to clean water is a universal human right.

definition: Without doubt; certainly
exampleSentence: The invention of the smartphone has undoubtedly changed the way we live.

definition: Lack of knowledge or information about something
exampleSentence: The campaign aims to dispel ignorance about mental health issues.

definition: A false perception or belief; something that deceives the mind
exampleSentence: Many people have the illusion that learning a language is quick and easy.

definition: A sudden strong urge to act without reflection
exampleSentence: The decision to change careers was driven by impulse rather than careful planning.

definition: Relating to a town or city; characteristic of city life
exampleSentence: Urban areas are experiencing rapid growth as more people move to cities.

definition: An arrival or entry of large numbers of people or things
exampleSentence: The influx of tourists has boosted the local economy but strained infrastructure.

definition: The ability to see into and understand the true nature of things
exampleSentence: The article offers valuable insight into the challenges facing the industry.

definition: An innate, natural impulse or behavior pattern
exampleSentence: Animals rely on instinct for survival rather than learned behavior.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
exampleSentence: The journalist's professional integrity was never questioned throughout her career.

definition: Intention or purpose behind an action
exampleSentence: The legislator's intent was to protect consumers from unfair business practices.

definition: A change or slight difference in condition, amount, or level
exampleSentence: There is considerable variation in the cost of living across different cities.

definition: Of very great extent, size, or amount; immense

definition: The amount of something taken in, such as food, air, or people admitted
exampleSentence: The university plans to increase its student intake by ten percent next year.

definition: A particular point in time or stage in a series of events

definition: Serving as a means of pursuing an aim or fulfilling a function
exampleSentence: The scholarship was instrumental in enabling her to attend university.

definition: Legally responsible; likely to do or experience something
exampleSentence: Companies are liable for damages caused by defective products.

definition: Relating to the edge of something; minor and barely significant
exampleSentence: The policy had only a marginal effect on reducing unemployment.

definition: Dull and ordinary; lacking interest or excitement
exampleSentence: The job involves both creative projects and mundane administrative tasks.

definition: So small or unimportant as to be not worth considering
exampleSentence: The difference between the two test results was negligible.

definition: Nearly; almost; by means of computer technology
exampleSentence: The cost of renewable energy has fallen virtually to the same level as fossil fuels.

definition: To a significant degree; noticeably
exampleSentence: The cost of living has increased appreciably over the past five years.

definition: In a manner that settles an issue or produces a definite result

definition: To an extent that is too large or too small in relation to something else
exampleSentence: Low-income families are disproportionately affected by cuts to public services.

definition: On the condition or understanding that

definition: Except if; in the case that something does not happen
exampleSentence: Unless the weather improves, the outdoor event will be cancelled.

definition: The amount of space occupied by a substance; the quantity or amount of something
exampleSentence: The volume of traffic on the roads has doubled in the last twenty years.

definition: Done or given of one's own free will; not required or forced
exampleSentence: Many students participate in voluntary community service during their holidays.

definition: Susceptible to physical or emotional harm or attack
exampleSentence: Elderly people are particularly vulnerable during extreme heatwaves.

definition: The health, prosperity, and happiness of a person or group
exampleSentence: The government's welfare programs provide support for low-income families.

definition: In contrast or comparison with the fact that
exampleSentence: Urban areas tend to have higher crime rates, whereas rural areas are generally safer.

definition: To remove or take away; to retreat or stop participating
exampleSentence: The company decided to withdraw its product from the market after safety concerns.

definition: Found or distributed over a large area or number of people
exampleSentence: There is widespread agreement among scientists that climate change is real.

definition: Ready, eager, or prepared to do something
exampleSentence: Employees must be willing to adapt to new technologies in the workplace.

definition: To remain undamaged or unaffected by; to resist
exampleSentence: Modern buildings are designed to withstand earthquakes and extreme weather.

definition: To see an event take place; to be present at something
exampleSentence: The past decade has witnessed remarkable advances in artificial intelligence.

definition: The people engaged in or available for work in a country or company

definition: To become or make something worse

definition: Sufficiently valuable or important to justify the time or effort spent
exampleSentence: Volunteering abroad can be a highly worthwhile experience for personal growth.

definition: To produce or provide a result, profit, or harvest
exampleSentence: The investment yielded a return of eight percent in the first year.

definition: An area or region with a specific characteristic or purpose
exampleSentence: The city has designated several pedestrian zones where cars are not allowed.

definition: Totally and completely; without any doubt
exampleSentence: The evidence absolutely proves that smoking causes lung cancer.

definition: To take in or soak up liquid, energy, or information
exampleSentence: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and release oxygen.

definition: The improper or harmful use of something; cruel treatment
exampleSentence: The charity aims to prevent drug abuse through education and support programs.

definition: To go somewhere with someone as a companion; to occur at the same time as
